item.title: Sunday dump of 14 woodworking amazing works I cannot do.
item.description:
Ever felt satisfied by wood ??
3. Lock and door latching concepts.
4. Incredible : Yes. Easy : Absolutely not.
5. Smoothest wood glue joint ever.
6. Incredible wood sculptures.
7. Super clean joinery demo.
8. The ASMR I didn’t know I needed.
9. woodworking techniques.
10. Cutting Wooden Logs into a spiral design.
11. Automated Woodworking.
12. Some stron glued joins
13. Just satisfying to watch.
14. Cutting wood thinner than paper.

Key West

item.title: All you need to know: Trump is a pedo
item.description:
There's no denying that Donald Trump is a pedophile. He's hung out with pedophiles the majority of his adult life. He needs to be held accountable.
And so does everybody else on that list. It doesn't matter their political affiliation, more status. They all need to be held accountable. FP edit: hey front page, pretty irritated today. My landlords, as I've mentioned before are 80 something Trump supporters. One of them, a Navy vet facing his Deathbed thinks that Mike Lindell could be a better governor in Minnesota then Tim Walz. They also recently posted about how illegal people should not be able to get socialized programs. While they themselves sign up to get socialized yard care. These people know what they're doing, they have no shame. And that's where we come in. Do not attack them, but shame them. Laugh at them. That has been my message all along. Do not let them be comfortable in society, they don't deserve to be. This does not mean go out of your way to harass. But if you have a family member who is openly supporting this rhetoric, correct them. See somebody with a red Trump hat in public shame them. These people, have gotten too comfortable abusing the rest of society. Do not let up, do not concede, do not comply.
item.dc:creator: JustGridSquares

This is quite dark I'm supprised it was ever made.
Yes, this is indeed and ad for a laptop
Felt appropriate to end with this
item.dc:creator: TypicalPoster

item.title: This might be terrible of me.
item.description: He was 75. Cancer got him. We'd all hoped he'd be around a good while longer; his dad had lived to 94, and died a few years ago. He was an LGBTQ+ activist for decades and went to some of the first Pride marches in Boston in the '70s, post Stonewall. Provided counseling for the queer community through some of the worst of the AIDS epidemic in the '80s. Told me he kept a suit hanging in his office in case he had to go to a funeral on short notice; that was an all too common occurrence back then. He was legally blind and retired at (IIRC) some point in the early '90s when his eyesight got too bad for him to work any more. I can imagine he was also mentally exhausted after doing that kind of work during the AIDS crisis. A most excellent person and as much as I miss him and wish he were still with us, I'm glad he doesn't have to put up with the misery the 47 administration is about to wreak on the LGBTQ+ community and on the disabled as well. May he rest in peace.
item.dc:creator: bikergeek6249

item.title: Heritage Foundation Protest MONDAYS Project 2025
item.description:
More needed to protest Project 2025 Heritage Foundation We outnumber them. Cliff Cash needs more to join before it's too late. Heritage Foundation, the authors of Project 2025, have their headquarters at 214 Massachusetts Avenue NE. It's a four minute walk from our 24/7 anti-fascist base camp in front of Union Station. Every Monday at 4pm, we greet the Nazis as they leave the office. They really, really hate it. Even Kevin Roberts is talking about our protests to the press. https://flareusa.cargo.site/ Apparently our protests are quite inconvenient for them. Good. After we walk them out, we have pizza at 6pm. Who do you know in the DMV that would like to get in on this? FuelTheFLARE #WeAreTheFlood #FLARE247DC
https://www.project2025.observer/
item.dc:creator: AnonymousMockingjay

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1425 (Star Trek Sunday)
item.description:
Let's set phasers to kill billionaires and take the means of bread production for ourselves.
Pippin tax. Today's message from Pippin: "What doing Pippin? WHAT DOING?" ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Chamngee nebo Karon!
item.description:
Good morning
Frank was once again ravenous. I give him his kitten food on the bathroom counter because of its on the floor, Puddles will hoover it.
Last night, Frank was a sleepy kitten.
But only after he was done playing. He he is with Puddles and Puddles' tail.
I swear Puddles knows what he's doing...
Bonus Puddles hanging out with dad.
Bonus Chessie mid-sneeze. Lazy Sunday today. Still haven't gotten out of bed proper. Should think about that and making breakfast. On Sundays when the tiniers are here, it's usually almond flour crepes. Need to see if we have strawberries. Hope you guys have a great day. Oh. Today's language is Tugen. It is a language spoken in Kenya, by a collection of tribes collectively known as the Kalenjin. Their history stretches back to nearly 6000 BCE, but they weren't known as the Kalenjin until the 1940s. If you're curious to know more, Wikipedia is a great jumping off point. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kalenjin_people
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten

item.title: Single for one single for all ❤️
item.dc:creator: zoraniko

item.title: Steve Buscemi did something amazing. On September 11, 2001, he showed up at his old firehouse in Manhattan, New York and was ready to help. Long before he became a movie star, Steve had served with Engine 55 in Manhattan, New York in the early 1980s.
item.description: Steve Buscemi did something amazing. On September 11, 2001, he showed up at his old firehouse in Manhattan, New York and was ready to help. Long before he became a movie star, Steve had served with Engine 55 in Manhattan, New York in the early 1980s. When the towers fell, he quietly returned to his old firehouse and joined the effort to search through the wreckage. For several days, Steve worked twelve-hour shifts alongside firefighters. He didn't want credit or cameras. He refused interviews, avoided photos and stayed in the background. All he cared about was helping other people in need. Even as smoke and debris filled the air around him, Steve dug through dust and rubble. But his kindness didn't end there. Steve helped raise money and gave his time to support firefighters. He works with a nonprofit called "Friends of Firefighters." They provide free counseling, therapy and support to firefighters and their families going through hard times. Steve Buscemi didn’t come back to be a hero. He came back because those were his people and they needed his help. ? (Photo: Steve Buscemi / A Good Job: Stories of the FDNY)
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: GovBrief Today - What Happened #177
item.description: The con is falling apart, but dangerous clowns still run the show. Trump's own State Department admits his refugee program is whites-only despite his public denials, while a teenager known as "Big Balls" helps eliminate federal regulations with AI. From Scotland, Trump distracts more from the Epstein circus by demanding his most recent opponent and the stars who endorsed her are prosecuted. The gap between what they say and what they do keeps widening. Worse, the people making these decisions keep getting more dangerous as they flail through their incompetence. Don't rely on the algorithm to keep you informed. Let me send this to you every night, free and private, so that you always know what's going on. Add your email at govbrief dot today, on Facebook, or on Substack at Gov Brief Today. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com 1. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-thailand-cambodia 2. https://go.govbrief.today/diplomats-asked-south-africa 3. https://go.govbrief.today/pentagon-lie-detector-program 4. https://go.govbrief.today/justice-dept-voter-rolls 5. https://go.govbrief.today/education-dept-releasing-funds 6. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-accuses-harris-endorsements 7. https://go.govbrief.today/doge-ai-cut-regulations Yesterday - https://go.govbrief.today/maxwell-more-interviews-immunity Fighting Back - https://go.govbrief.today/trump-scotland-protests Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos
